title: GUIDELINES FOR THE RECOGNITION OF ACTIVE SUBSTANCES OR AUTHORIZED USES OF ACTIVE SUBSTANCES OF LOW PUBLIC HEALTH CONCERN THAT ARE CONSIDERED EXEMPTED FROM THE ESTABLISHMENT OF MAXIMUM RESIDUE LIMITS OR DO NOT GIVE RISE TO RESIDUES

sections.section: GUIDELINES FOR THE RECOGNITION OF ACTIVE SUBSTANCES OR AUTHORIZED USES OF ACTIVE SUBSTANCES OF LOW PUBLIC HEALTH CONCERN THAT ARE CONSIDERED EXEMPTED FROM THE ESTABLISHMENT OF MAXIMUM RESIDUE LIMITS OR DO NOT GIVE RISE TO RESIDUES
sections.summary: Document title: guidelines for recognizing active substances or authorized uses of active substances of low public health concern that may be exempt from establishing MRLs or do not give rise to residues.

Preface defining pesticides (chemical and biological), biopesticides and related terms, explaining the role of MRLs and JMPR, and stating these guidelines aim to harmonize criteria for exempting active substances or specific uses of low public health concern from the need to establish MRLs based on toxicology and residue behaviour.

Specifies the guidelines’ scope: to provide a consistent, harmonized approach for deciding when an active substance or a specific authorized use can be considered exempt from establishing MRLs, notes they do not cover certain uses (e.g., some seed treatments), and are intended for competent authorities lacking their own exemption criteria.

Provides definitions of terms relevant to MRL exemption decisions, including ADI, ARfD, active substance, 'low public health concern', authorized use, biopesticide/botanical/microbial/natural substances, GAP, JMPR, MRL/CXL, pesticide residue, semiochemicals (and SCLPs), feed, food/crop groups, pests and environmental exposure.
